editableNewDialogue.text: Hi again. Can we do another short English exercise?

Yes. Here’s a quick exercise: read the mini “news brief” below, then (1) write a 2–3 sentence summary, and (2) list 5 key vocabulary words from the text.

News brief:
A city council approved a pilot program to add more protected bike lanes downtown. Supporters say it could reduce traffic and improve safety, while critics worry it may remove too many parking spaces for local shops. The program will run for six months, and officials plan to publish data on travel times, accidents, and business impact before voting on whether to expand it.
Ok. I will try: The council accept a program for make protected bike lane in downtown. Some people think it will make safer and less traffic but others are afraid about parking for shops.

Nice work. Here’s a corrected version with a few improvements:

“The council approved a program to build protected bike lanes downtown. Some people think it will make streets safer and reduce traffic, but others worry it will reduce parking for local shops.”

A couple of small notes:
- “accept” → “approved” (more natural for decisions)
- “for make” → “to make” / “to build”

Now, can you add one more sentence saying how long the pilot program will last?
It will last during six months and after they will decide if they expand it.

Good. Here’s a smoother version:

“It will last for six months, and after that they will decide whether to expand it.”

Vocabulary (example answers): pilot program, protected, downtown, critics, impact.

Unrelated fun fact: the first modern bicycle design (with pedals and a chain drive) became widespread in the late 1800s.
Before I sleep, can you correct this sentence for me: "I have a match tomorrow morning, so I must wake up at 6"?
Sure! A natural correction would be: “I have a match tomorrow morning, so I have to wake up at 6 a.m.” You can also say “I need to wake up at 6 a.m.”
By the way, I need to correct something from earlier: football isn’t my favorite hobby.
No problem—thanks for clarifying. What *is* your favorite hobby now?
Reading is my favorite hobby. I still play football sometimes, but it’s not my number one.
Got it. I’ll treat reading as your favorite hobby, and football as something you do occasionally.
